Institute Identity

Upload the institute icon, preview it live, and set a clear institute nickname for the sidebar brand.

This nickname appears clearly in the sidebar brand and stays separate from the official institution name.
PNG, JPG, WEBP, or SVG. Save the institute profile to keep it.
Optional. This appears under the institute nickname only when you enter a value.
Fit keeps the full icon visible. Crop fills the icon box, and zoom lets you frame it.

Student Information

Manually register students with the live header structure, grouped form sections, and custom field setup already configured for SMS.

Registered Student Edit Flow

Matches the PDF student-information flow for opening a registered student and editing section-level information safely.

  • Use Registered Students to open an existing student record.
  • Edit section values and save changes back into the SMS register.
Add Student with UMIS / EMIS Context

Covers both manual intake and students who already exist on UMIS with EMIS-backed lookup context.

  • Manual entry stays available in this workspace for direct student creation.
  • Imported or existing-student pathways continue through the import and register workspaces.
Verification and Scholarship Actions

Community, first graduate, income verification, plus Agree or Appeal actions are aligned to the Scholarship module.

Manage institute master details, contact profile, head of institute, and nodal officer information in one place.

Create Batch

Start with the batch identity, upload source file, and define how imported rows should match fetched student records.

These are batch defaults. Step 2 overrides transfer behavior per field. Step 3 refines match behavior.

Step Goal prepare a safe import draft
Give this batch a unique, descriptive name for easy identification
Batch name must be unique before transfer.
Optional: Add details about the source, timing, or purpose of this import
Sets the batch-wide default transfer behavior. Step 2 Field Mapping can override this per mapped field when needed.
Sets the batch-wide default match design. Step 3 Keying can keep this default or override it with custom key sets and quick setups.
Run validation to see exact and fuzzy match confidence by row.

Choose UMIS Excel or CSV File

Supports .xlsx, .xls and .csv. Data will be validated before transfer.

Auto-maps headers with suggestions, allows overrides before import
No file selected
Ready to analyze import file.
Analyze a file to profile schema drift and auto-map confidence.

Recover Saved Batch

Reopen an old draft, inspect a completed batch, or revive an earlier configuration as a new wizard draft.

Keying Workspace

Choose the import key for record identity, choose the duplicate match key for review, then open Advanced Setup only when this batch needs custom tuning.

Import Key controls routing. Duplicate Match Key controls warnings and review groups.

Keying - Step 3 override
Keying features: import identity routing, duplicate review strategy, saved presets, workspace defaults, composite key sets, and advanced batch-specific overrides. Simple mode handles the standard flow. Advanced Setup keeps the heavier tools available when needed.

Advanced Duplicate Review

Review duplicate groups and existing-record collisions using the active keying model. Resolve conflicts manually, apply batch rules, and customize row handling before validation. Use active keying or apply a duplicate-specific exact key to regroup conflicts.

Active keying: Using batch default

Note: Duplicate override only changes Step 4 conflict grouping and review. It does not replace Step 3 student identity matching.

Duplicates - Manual conflict control
Duplicate Match Override

Use an additional exact duplicate-review key when the active keying model is too broad or not specific enough for conflict grouping.

This only affects Step 4 duplicate grouping and review. It does not replace student matching in Step 3.
Duplicate Match Strategy only affects scanning when Duplicate Override Mode is set to "Use duplicate-specific key".
Duplicate review is currently using the active Step 3 keying model.
Duplicate Groups

Review collisions using the active keying logic and decide how the batch should treat them.

Duplicate Audit Table

Audit-ready table of duplicate groups and existing-record collisions after manual resolution decisions.

Group Matched Key Rows Resolution
Analyzed Data Preview

Sample rows from the uploaded file so you can inspect structure before matching and duplicate review.

Mapping Snapshot

Review the current source to target mapping set without leaving the wizard flow.

Field Mapping Rules

Map source fields to canonical fields and override transfer behavior where needed.

Uses the Step 1 default transfer rule unless a field-level mapping override is configured.

GLOBAL COLLEGE ERP

Realtime Operations Fabric

Live cross-module health for academic, finance, student success, campus operations, governance, compliance, and platform services.

Waiting for live state...

Module Registry

Each module stays centrally governed, AI-aware, and ready for customization from the hub.

Module Record Studio

Create live operational records for any workspace module without leaving this integrated workspace.

Choose a module to load its operational fields.

Operational Records

Focused view of the selected module with quick edit and cleanup controls.

Automation Designer

Create cross-module rules such as low attendance escalation, fee recovery triggers, and placement nudges.

Student Workflow Engine

Link fee dues, attendance risk, exam readiness, and scholarship actions directly to students.

Pick a student and module to build a linked workflow.

Student Impact Board

Latest workflow status per student with eligibility and escalation effects calculated across modules.

Finance Ledger

Student-wise dues, recoveries, and payment pressure from the latest fee workflows.

Attendance Register

Risk-aware operational attendance board with threshold tracking and action prompts.

Exam Eligibility Board

Combined finance and attendance decisioning for exam readiness and condonation review.

Scholarship Action Desk

Students who should be reviewed for scholarship support or disbursement follow-up.

Cross-Module Signals

At-risk snapshots linking student master data with workspace operations and AI-ready thresholds.

Recent Activity

Traceability for module changes, rule updates, and control hub decisions.

Academic Years & Backups

Control the active academic year, maintain the local year register, and protect workspace state with restorable snapshots.

Academic Year Register

Add new, current, or historical academic-year timelines.

Backup Snapshots

Select or restore academic-year snapshots saved from this workspace.

Activity Trace

Control the top-bar trace widget, its collapse behavior, retention limit, and which event categories are captured.

Capture Categories
The top-bar widget follows these settings after you save them.
